cataloging_subject.label: Altenglische Dichtungen des MS. Harl. 2253 (Böddeker’s 45-text selection)
cataloging_subject.scope_note: The cataloged intellectual unit is Karl Böddeker’s exact forty-five-text edited selection from British Library Harley MS 2253 as printed on pp. 95-300 of his 1878 volume and serialized in U-M/General Corpus afy7793. It includes eight political poems, fourteen secular lyrics, eighteen religious lyrics, the Debate between Body and Soul, Maximian, the Life of Saint Marina, the Harrowing of Hell, and the Proverbs of Hendyng, together with Böddeker’s interleaved German headnotes and apparatus. It is not the complete manuscript, not every Middle English text in Harley 2253, not the complete 1878 book, and not a single authored medieval work.

catalog_summary.title: Altenglische Dichtungen des MS. Harl. 2253 (Böddeker’s 45-text selection)
catalog_summary.author: Anonymous authors of forty-five heterogeneous medieval texts
catalog_summary.date: Texts individually composed before their attestation in a Harley 2253 component copied circa 1331-1341; exact edited selection first published in 1878
catalog_summary.region: Selected witnesses copied in or near Ludlow in the West Midlands; individual composition places unresolved; edition published in Berlin

summaries.value: An edited selection of forty-five anonymous Middle English verse texts from British Library Harley MS 2253, arranged by Karl Böddeker in 1878 into political, secular, religious, debate, hagiographic, dramatic, and proverbial groupings. The historical texts range from political songs and love lyrics to Marian devotion, Passion meditation, body-soul debate, saint’s life, verse drama, and wisdom literature. Böddeker’s substantial German prose headnotes and textual notes are part of the cataloged editorial unit but remain distinct from the medieval works.

The source body contains three thematic DIV1 sections holding 8, 14, and 18 DIV2 poems, followed by five standalone DIV1 works: Debate between Body and Soul, Maximian, the Life of Saint Marina, the Harrowing of Hell, and the Proverbs of Hendyng. The forty-five historical items contain 4,027 encoded L elements and 474 LG elements; 201 body P elements carry interleaved editorial prose.

The exact selected unit occupies Böddeker printed pp. 95-300. It excludes the German introduction and grammar (roman pp. i-xvi and Arabic pp. 1-92), the section half-title and blank pp. 93-94, the glossary (pp. 301-451), appendix (pp. 452-463), and correction page 464. The chosen texts occupy discontiguous Harley folios 55v-128v, and Böddeker’s political/secular/religious order differs substantially from manuscript order.

Böddeker worked directly from Harley 2253 but also compared many earlier editions and parallel texts, occasionally recording or adopting an earlier editor’s reading. The graph therefore treats Harley as the direct source witness and Böddeker as transcriber/editor/collator, without flattening every cited edition into one common upstream source. His discussion of the Digby 86 parallel was mediated by E. Stengel and discovered only after the selected text had already been printed; the comparison appendix is outside afy7793.

U-M describes the 2006 electronic text as uncorrected OCR with no content editing, encoded at TEI in Libraries Level 1 and linked historically to 484 page images. The repository XML retains the forty-five selected texts but represents excluded front and back matter mainly as page-break placeholders. Current automated access to the U-M resolver encounters a Cloudflare challenge, and no challenge page is cached as source evidence.

extent.description: Forty-five historical verse works organized in forty-eight XML content divisions: three thematic DIV1 containers with forty DIV2 poems, plus five standalone DIV1 works. The body contains 4,027 L, 474 LG, 201 P, 45 MILESTONE, and 207 PB elements. The PB count includes an unnumbered leaf before printed p. 95; the target printed selection is pp. 95-300. The P elements and embedded NOTE1 elements are editorial apparatus, not additional historical works. Counts describe the OCR/XML representation rather than settled canonical lineation.

content_parts.label: Böddeker’s German headnotes and textual apparatus
content_parts.summary: Substantial German introductions, historical and philological commentary, manuscript readings, source comparisons, and footnotes interleaved with the forty-five Middle English texts.

locators.value: BODY//ARGUMENT/P, BODY//P, and NOTE1; 201 body P elements plus embedded notes

notes: This virtual part separates the edition’s prose editorial layer from the forty-five historical verse works; it is not an additional medieval item.

content_parts.label: Political songs
content_parts.summary: Eight thematically grouped political and social poems; Böddeker’s order is editorial rather than manuscript order.

content_parts.label: Secular songs
content_parts.summary: Fourteen secular lyrics on love, spring, satire, longing, and popular or comic subjects.

content_parts.label: Religious songs
content_parts.summary: Eighteen religious, devotional, penitential, Marian, and moral lyrics.

content_parts.label: A Song of Lewes
content_parts.summary: Political song on the Battle of Lewes and Richard of Cornwall, in eight seven-line stanzas with a refrain.

related_works.title: British Library Harley MS 2253 miscellany
notes: The forty-five selected witnesses are discontiguous parts of the manuscript; the cataloged Böddeker selection is not identical to the complete codex or its order.

related_works.title: Harley Lyrics (selections)
notes: The two General Corpus selections overlap in thirty METS articles. HarLyr alone includes article 24b; afy7793 adds fifteen articles absent from HarLyr.

evidence.summary: Primary evidence for the U-M title and identifier, 1878 source statement, 2006 OCR and encoding declaration, exact body selection, omitted-matter placeholders, headings, languages, and editorial apparatus.

evidence.citation: Namespace-free structural audit of afy7793.xml against the 1878 scan and the METS/DIMEV item maps.
evidence.locator: BODY/DIV1, DIV2, HEAD, ARGUMENT, P, LG, L, NOTE1, MILESTONE, and PB
evidence.summary: Verifies 45 historical items in three thematic containers plus five standalone works; counts 4,027 L, 474 LG, 201 P, 45 MILESTONE, and 207 body PB elements; and maps every item to print pages, Harley folios, METS articles, and DIMEV records.

evidence.citation: Karl Böddeker, Altenglische Dichtungen des MS. Harl. 2253: Mit Grammatik und Glossar (Berlin: Weidmann, 1878), complete PIMS/Internet Archive scan and OCR.
evidence.locator: Title page; introduction; contents; printed pp. 93-301; appendix and corrections
evidence.summary: Direct evidence for the complete 1878 volume, exact pp. 95-300 selection boundary, Böddeker’s thematic arrangement, item headings, historical and philological commentary, direct use of Harley 2253, cited earlier editions, and excluded surrounding matter.

evidence.citation: British Library, Archives and Manuscripts, Harley MS 2253 parent record and relevant second-part child record.
evidence.locator: Composite codex; relevant ff. 49r-142v; selected item locators; date, place, script, access, and digital-coverage statements
evidence.summary: Establishes the physical manuscript and relevant part, circa 1330-1340 current catalog date, near-Ludlow/West Midlands context, discontiguous target folios, restricted physical access, and currently unavailable official images.

evidence.citation: Susanna Fein, ed. and trans., with David Raybin and Jan Ziolkowski, The Complete Harley 2253 Manuscript, 3 vols. (2014-2015), combined METS PDF.
evidence.locator: Articles 21-23, 25, 25a, 27-36, 40-41, 43-53, 58, 60-69, 73-74, 81, 88-89, and 92-93; appendix of manuscript contents
evidence.summary: Later independent scholarly edition and translation used to verify exact item titles, article numbers, current folio locators, manuscript order, genre distinctions, and the circa 1331-1341 copying frame. It is collateral, not upstream of afy7793.

evidence.citation: Digital Index of Middle English Verse, version 1.0 dataset (Zenodo, 2025), Records.xml, Manuscripts.xml, and Bibliography.xml.
evidence.locator: Forty-five BLHar2253 witness rows carrying edition key Boddeker1878; manuscript BLHar2253; bibliography Boddeker1878
evidence.summary: Independently confirms exactly forty-five Harley 2253 witnesses cited to Böddeker 1878 and supplies record identifiers, incipits, descriptions, folio locators, and edition page locators.

evidence.citation: General Corpus work metadata manifest metadata:HarLyr.
evidence.locator: cataloging subject, content part, and METS article mapping
evidence.summary: Supports the exact thirty-item overlap between afy7793 and the separate thirty-one-item HarLyr edited selection, while preserving their distinct editorial boundaries.

evidence.locator: entities, relations, access, rights, editorial practices, evidence, and open questions
evidence.summary: Companion graph establishing the repository/U-M/Böddeker/Harley chain, practical scan and facsimile routes, later scholarly controls, cache inventory, layered rights, and unresolved access questions.

open_questions.question: What narrower authorship, composition date, composition place, alternate-witness, and textual-family claims can be established for each of the forty-five historical works?
next_steps: Research each DIMEV record and METS article against current item-level scholarship.
Keep future claims item-scoped rather than assigning one context to the entire selection.

open_questions.question: Can authorized current images of all Harley target folios, or a lawful complete digital copy of Ker’s 1965 facsimile, be obtained with clear reuse terms?
next_steps: Use the British Library imaging service with a complete-object request and a separate target-folio map.
Ask EETS and holding libraries about licensed digital or physical access to OS 255.
Recheck the BL catalog when its advertised digital coverage is restored.

open_questions.question: Can the 2006 U-M OCR and its historically linked 484 TIFF page images be accessed without the current automated Cloudflare challenge, and what version history connects them to the repository XML?
next_steps: Verify the resolver in a normal browser or ask U-M Digital Collections for an item-specific access route.
Request conversion/version records and image identifiers from U-M.
Do not cache challenge pages or infer that the metadata-only Internet Archive item contains the target.

open_questions.question: What item-specific reuse terms govern the U-M OCR/XML and page images, separately from the public-domain 1878 edition and the CME repository’s software-only Unlicense?
next_steps: Seek written source-specific clarification from U-M and the CME maintainer before broad redistribution.
Keep medieval wording, Böddeker edition, scan derivatives, OCR, modern editions, and repository artifact rights separate.

notes: The catalog boundary follows the exact forty-five-text editorial unit represented in the BODY, not the broadest title string, full print volume, or physical manuscript.
All forty-five historical items are verse; the whole unit is mixed because Böddeker’s prose headnotes and apparatus are substantial and retained.
Thematic print order must not be mistaken for manuscript order. Exact current folio and METS article locators are recorded item by item.

The XML profile declares German because the edition frame is German; direct inspection and modern controls establish Middle English as the historical texts’ primary language and Anglo-Norman in one bilingual item.
Böddeker’s circa-1310 dating and Leominster framing are not adopted wholesale; current BL/METS evidence is scoped to the witness component and preserves uncertainty.

Rights and access claims are source-specific in the companion lineage manifest; public-domain medieval wording or 1878 editorial matter does not automatically determine rights in later photographs, OCR, metadata, or editions.
